_comment: Per-backend regression pins. Each entry pins (a) the GGUF under test to a specific HF revision SHA so an upstream re-quantise doesn't silently regress us, and (b) the reference-dump archive in cstr/crispasr-regression-fixtures (also pinned). See tests/regression/README.md. Entries with skip_diff=true run transcript-only checks (no ref dump needed yet); see README for the fast-track workflow.

fixtures._note: Pinned. To re-bake, run `tools/kaggle-regression.py` with MODE=rebake UPLOAD=1; the new commit SHA gets pasted here as a reviewable diff. Previous: 99f005d59a0f7f4915dea5faea4d2e17b2120268 (2026-05-17). Bumped 2026-05-25 after Kaggle rebake v13 published 9 ref archives — adds parakeet-tdt-1.1b, parakeet-tdt_ctc-1.1b, parakeet-rnnt-0.6b, parakeet-rnnt-1.1b (all NEW from handover-prompts/extend-coverage-via-kaggle.md realistic goal); refreshes canary-1b-v2, moonshine-tiny/base, parakeet-tdt-0.6b-{en,ja}.

backends.expected_transcript: ピッチャーの岡本は1回戦の鳥取城北戦は8回、6対33点リードの場面で3人目でマウンドに上がりました。

NOT the human ground-truth (ground_truth.srt in the fixtures repo, which reads 山本 / Yamamoto rather than the model's ピッチャーの岡本 / Okamoto). Lock-in is for regression, not correctness.
backends.diff_thresholds_note: mel_spectrogram tightened from 0.95 to 0.999 after drop_last_frame fix (commit 07cfcff). NeMo ref needs rebake to clip mel to feat_len; current ref has one extra frame that the comparison truncates.

backends.transcript_tolerance_note: glm-asr-nano's LLM-style decoder ties on score-boundary punctuation/case ('you. Ask' vs 'you, ask') — the encoder+mel checks BOTH pass at cos=1.000 (model weights are identical) but a runner/library upgrade can nudge a softmax score by 1e-7 and flip a decode tie. 2% CER / 5% WER catches real regressions (missing/wrong words, language confusion) while tolerating cosmetic decode drift. Other backends keep the tighter byte-equal bar by omitting this field.
backends.diff_thresholds_note: F32 GGUF vs Python reference gives cos_min=1.000 for both mel and encoder.

tts_backends_note: TTS->ASR roundtrip regression. Each entry synthesises tts_phrase via the TTS backend, transcribes the output with the pinned ASR backend (parakeet by default — high-accuracy English ground truth), and asserts WER <= wer_max. Catches silent decode regressions like the voxcpm2 BF16 noise bug that the encoder/mel diff harness can miss. WER ignores punctuation and case after normalisation, so a kokoro tts_phrase that comes back as 'the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.' is still WER=0.

tts_backends.name: kokoro-82m-en
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: Kokoro 82M Q8_0 on a clean pangram routinely lands at WER 0.0 in local roundtrip via parakeet. 10% gives headroom for prosody artefacts + parakeet's own ~0% baseline drift on the CI runner image.

tts_backends.name: qwen3-tts-0.6b-customvoice-eric
tts_backends.voice_preset_note: qwen3-tts-CustomVoice has 9 baked speakers selectable by name (aiden, dylan, eric, ono_anna, ryan, serena, sohee, uncle_fu, vivian). `eric` is a clear male voice — best fit for the pangram phrase + parakeet transcription. No separate voice GGUF needed; the speaker embed lives inside the customvoice model.
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: Local roundtrip lands at WER 0.0 (parakeet adds a trailing '.' which normalisation strips). The Q8_0 model is used here; Q4_K has a documented quality regression on this same repo (see its README) so we pin Q8_0 explicitly. Total download per nightly slot: 970 MB talker + 360 MB tokenizer + 466 MB parakeet = ~1.8 GB.

tts_backends.name: bark-small
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: Bark is a generative audio model with variable output quality. 20% WER headroom for prosody artefacts.

tts_backends.name: csm-1b
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: CSM-1B Q4_K conversational TTS. 15% WER headroom.

tts_backends.name: speecht5-tts-en
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: SpeechT5 80M deterministic TTS. Needs x-vector for speaker conditioning.

tts_backends.name: piper-lessac-en
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: Piper VITS ~30MB deterministic TTS. 22 kHz output.

tts_backends.name: pocket-tts-en
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: Kyutai Pocket TTS 100M, continuous-latent AR at 12.5 Hz, CC-BY-4.0.

tts_backends.name: f5-tts-v1-base
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: F5-TTS DiT flow-matching. Needs reference audio for voice cloning; without --voice uses built-in default.

tts_backends.name: parler-tts-mini-v1.1
tts_backends.tts_phrase: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
tts_backends.wer_max_note: Parler TTS Mini v1.1 (~900M). T5 encoder + DAC decoder. Prompt-conditioned voice via --instruct.

tts_backends.name: dia-1.6b
tts_backends.tts_phrase: [S1] The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
tts_backends._tts_phrase_note: Dia requires [S1] or [S2] speaker tags. Prompts under ~100 chars may produce poor output.
tts_backends.wer_max_note: Dia 1.6B dialogue TTS with CFG. 20% WER headroom for stochastic decode.
